ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn term exists specifically for tuna mayo sandwich on wholemeal bread; closest parent term 'sandwich food product' (FOODON_00002473) used as the most defensible anchor.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A0CKP (Sandwich) used as the closest defensible category; no specific wholemeal tuna mayo sandwich code exists in FoodEx2.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No specific SNOMED CT code identified for tuna mayo sandwich; omitted to preserve ontology honesty.

serving.description: 1 sandwich (2 slices wholemeal bread with tuna mayo filling)
serving.natural_language_portion: One typical serving is one sandwich made with two slices of wholemeal bread and a standard tuna mayonnaise filling, weighing approximately 150 g, providing around 303 kcal.

nutrient_source.title: Tuna Mayo Sandwich (Wholemeal) — Singapore HPB Foodo Nutrient Database
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values taken directly from HPB Foodo entry for Tuna Mayo Sandwich (Wholemeal), cr_id F05020117, with confidence 0.95. All core macros and secondary nutrients are sourced from this entry.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2008 — Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Diabetes Care Journal
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for tuna mayo sandwich on wholemeal bread. GI estimated at 65 based on wholemeal bread GI of 65 (University of Sydney GI Database; Atkinson et al. 2008, Diabetes Care). The tuna and mayonnaise filling has negligible carbohydrate content and does not materially alter the GI of the composite dish. Glycemic load per 150 g serving calculated as: GI (65) × available carbohydrate per serving (24.05 g × 1.5 = 36.1 g) / 100 = 23.5.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium 408 mg per 100 g — marginally exceeds the 400 mg/100 g threshold for hypertension-safe foods
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit portion size to one sandwich and choose low-sodium canned tuna and reduced-sodium mayonnaise to bring the sodium content below 400 mg per 100 g; the dietary fibre (3.6 g/100 g) and potassium from wholemeal bread provide partial cardiovascular offset but do not fully compensate for the elevated sodium.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Moderate GI (~65) and 24 g carbohydrate per 100 g; wholemeal bread fibre (3.6 g/100 g) and high protein (12.1 g/100 g) attenuate postprandial glucose response
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with Type 2 Diabetes can include this sandwich in a balanced meal plan, but should monitor portion size — a standard 150 g serving delivers approximately 36 g total carbohydrate — and pair it with non-starchy vegetables to further reduce the glycaemic impact; choosing mayonnaise without added sugar is also recommended.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Sodium 408 mg/100 g is elevated; however, tuna provides cardioprotective omega-3 fatty acids (EPA/DHA) and saturated fat is low (~1.3 g/100 g)
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: For individuals with cardiovascular disease, the omega-3 content from tuna is beneficial for lipid profile and inflammation, but the sodium level (408 mg/100 g) warrants caution — use low-sodium tuna and reduced-fat mayonnaise, and limit to one sandwich per meal to keep total sodium intake within daily targets of less than 2000 mg.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Tuna provides EPA/DHA omega-3 fatty acids with established anti-inflammatory properties; saturated fat is low and trans fat is absent
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: This sandwich is generally suitable for individuals with rheumatoid arthritis due to the anti-inflammatory omega-3 content of tuna; however, limit canned tuna to 2–3 servings per week due to mercury content, and monitor for individual sensitivity to nightshade ingredients if tomato is included in the filling.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Tuna Mayo Sandwich (Wholemeal) is a popular Western-style sandwich widely consumed in Singapore as a breakfast, school canteen, and lunchbox item. It consists of two slices of wholemeal bread filled with a mixture of canned tuna and mayonnaise, sometimes supplemented with lettuce, tomato, or cucumber. The wholemeal bread provides a slightly nutty, dense texture compared to white bread, and contributes meaningful dietary fibre (3.6 g per 100 g) that moderates glucose absorption and supports digestive health. The tuna mayonnaise filling delivers high protein (12.1 g per 100 g) and omega-3 fatty acids (EPA and DHA) from the tuna, which have established anti-inflammatory and cardioprotective benefits. The mayonnaise contributes fat (6.7 g per 100 g total fat, approximately 1.3 g saturated fat) and a creamy, mild flavour. Energy density is moderate at 202 kcal per 100 g. Sodium is elevated at 408 mg per 100 g due to the combined contribution of canned tuna, mayonnaise, and wholemeal bread, making this a borderline food for individuals managing hypertension. The glycaemic index is estimated at approximately 65 (medium), based on the wholemeal bread component. A standard serving of 150 g provides approximately 303 kcal, 18 g protein, 36 g carbohydrate, and 10 g fat. This food is suitable for most healthy adults and individuals with Type 2 Diabetes when portion-controlled, but sodium management is important for those with hypertension or cardiovascular disease. It is not suitable for individuals with fish or egg allergies, and contains gluten.
common_user_queries: How many calories in a tuna sandwich on wholemeal bread?
Is tuna sandwich good for diabetics?
Can I eat tuna sandwich if I have high blood pressure?
What is the protein content of a tuna mayo sandwich?
Is wholemeal tuna sandwich high in sodium?
Is tuna mayo sandwich suitable for weight loss?
What is the GI of a wholemeal tuna sandwich?

confidence_reason: Nutrient values sourced directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo database (cr_id F05020117) with a recorded confidence of 0.95. This is an exact match from the primary approved regional source for Singapore foods. GI is estimated from the wholemeal bread component using the University of Sydney GI Database, which is the approved GI source.
notes: monounsaturated_fat_g and polyunsaturated_fat_g set to -1: detailed fatty acid breakdown for this composite sandwich is not reported in the HPB Foodo database entry F05020117. potassium_mg, cholesterol_mg, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, and water_g set to -1: these micronutrient and moisture values are not reported in the HPB Foodo source for this entry. trans_fat_g set to 0.0: trans fat is confirmed absent in this food based on the ingredient profile (wholemeal bread, canned tuna, standard mayonnaise); confirmed negligible per HPB source context. saturated_fat_g of 1.3 g per 100 g is estimated from the fat composition of the known ingredients (canned tuna ~0.3 g sat fat per 100 g, mayonnaise ~1.5 g sat fat per 15 g serving, wholemeal bread ~0.3 g sat fat per 100 g) and is consistent with the HPB total fat value of 6.71 g per 100 g. GI of 65 is an analogous estimate based on wholemeal bread GI from the University of Sydney GI Database; no direct GI measurement for this composite sandwich was found. GL per 150 g serving calculated as 65 × (24.05 × 1.5) / 100 = 23.5. Although this food is commonly consumed in Singapore, it is a Western-style dish and not a traditional Singaporean ethnic dish; accordingly, local-language names in Chinese, Malay, Tamil, and Hindi have been provided as contextual translations to support multilingual retrieval in the Singapore food environment, rather than as mandatory ethnic-dish names. The Chinese simplified name 金枪鱼蛋黄酱三明治（全麦）and traditional name 鮪魚美乃滋三明治（全麥）are direct translations. The Malay name Sandwic tuna mayo (roti gandum penuh) uses standard Malay food terminology. The Tamil name டுனா மயோ சாண்ட்விச் (முழு கோதுமை) and Hindi name टूना मेयो सैंडविच (होलमील) are transliterations with descriptive translations appropriate for the Singapore multilingual food context.
